SEARCH
Package p2main

Class VotingAlgorithm

java.lang.Object
p2main.VotingAlgorithm
Direct Known Subclasses:
MunicipalAlgorithm, PluralityAlgorithm, STVAlgorithm
public abstract class VotingAlgorithm extends Object
Abstract class representing a voting algorithm. This class defines the structure for any voting algorithm, including handling election data, processing results and displaying it on console.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    protected Election
    election
    The election associated with this voting algorithm.
    List<String>
    loserList
    List of losers determined by the voting algorithm.
    int
    numWinners
    Number of winners to be selected in the election.
    List<String>
    winnerList
    List of winners determined by the voting algorithm.

  • Field Details

    • election

      protected Election election
      The election associated with this voting algorithm.

    • winnerList

      public List<String> winnerList
      List of winners determined by the voting algorithm.

    • loserList

      public List<String> loserList
      List of losers determined by the voting algorithm.

    • numWinners

      public int numWinners
      Number of winners to be selected in the election.

  • Constructor Details

    • VotingAlgorithm

      public VotingAlgorithm()
      Constructor for VotingAlgorithm. Initializes the winner and loser lists.

  • Method Details

    • runAlgorithm

      public abstract void runAlgorithm(List<Ballot> ballots)
      Runs the voting algorithm on the given list of ballots. Implementations must define how the algorithm processes votes.
      Parameters:
      ballots - List of ballots cast in the election.

    • displayResults

      protected abstract void displayResults()
      Displays the results of the election. Implementations must define how results are print to console.

    • breakTie

      protected abstract List<Integer> breakTie(List<Integer> tieList)
      Breaks a tie between candidates in case of equal votes. Implementations must define a method to resolve ties.
      Parameters:
      tieList - List of candidate indices involved in a tie.
      Returns:
      A list of candidate indices ordered based on tie resolution.
